title: leucas stachydiformis
intro: Leucas stachydiformis, also known as wild costus or kalmegh, is a perennial plant native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is known to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and pain, treating digestive issues, and supporting liver and kidney function. Leucas stachydiformis can be prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or oil, and is often used in traditional remedies for its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
